prerequisites: A reachable Unleash instance (Unleash Enterprise, hosted, or self-hosted Open Source) whose base URL you can supply as `api_url`. Unleash exposes its Admin API under `<your-unleash-url>/api/admin`.

An Unleash API token supplied as `api_token`. Generating backend or frontend tokens via Admin settings > Access control > API access requires the Admin root role or a custom root role with API token permissions.
Knowledge of which environment and project(s) the token should cover, since every Unleash API token is scoped to one or more projects and a single environment.
If you want to limit extraction to a single project, the project ID/name to set as the optional `project_name` field.

authMethods.name: API token (Authorization header)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the connector's `api_token` secret field. Unleash authenticates every request by placing a valid API token directly in the `Authorization` header with no `Bearer` prefix (for example `Authorization: YOUR_TOKEN`). A token has the form `{projects}:{environment}.{hash}`, where projects is a single project ID, a list, or `*` for all projects. Use a token that can read the resources you need: a service account token or personal access token (which inherits the creator's permissions), or the deprecated admin token that grants full read and write access to all projects, environments, and root resources.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Unleash instance URL
setupSteps.body: Determine the base URL of your Unleash instance (hosted or self-hosted), for example `https://your-unleash-instance.com`. The Admin API used to read data lives under `<your-unleash-url>/api/admin`. You will provide this base URL as the required `api_url` config field.

setupSteps.title: Create an API token in the Unleash Admin UI
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Unleash with an account that has the Admin root role (or a custom root role with API token permissions). Go to Admin settings > Access control > API access to create a backend or frontend token, or use Admin settings > Service accounts to create a service account token. Alternatively, create a Personal API token under Profile > View profile settings > Personal API tokens, or a project-scoped token under a project's Settings > API access. Choose the environment and project scope appropriate for what you want to extract.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set `api_token` to the token value generated above and `api_url` to your Unleash instance base URL. Unleash will accept the token via the `Authorization` header on each request (no `Bearer` prefix).

setupSteps.title: Configure optional scoping fields
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `project_name` to restrict extraction to a single Unleash project (otherwise the token's project scope applies). Optionally set `nameprefix` to apply a prefix to the records or streams produced. Both fields are optional and can be left blank.

sources.title: API tokens and client keys - Unleash Documentation
